target.name: Porotech
target.founder: Tongtong Zhu, Yingjun Liu, Rachel Oliver

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $20M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Porotech is a Series A semiconductor company founded by Tongtong Zhu, Yingjun Liu, and Rachel Oliver. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 8 private and 3 public peers in Chips & Semiconductors, including Substrate, Rain AI, and Etched at the private level, and ALAB, NVDA, and AMD as public anchors.

Two triangulation methods apply to this stage. Stage-progression analysis uses the company's $20M+ total raised as a base and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement, implying a $40M valuation. Peer-median analysis across four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies yields a $1.0B median, with a $500M–$5.0B range: Etched trades at $5.0B (Series B), Substrate and Positron AI each at $1.0B (Series A and B respectively), and Rain AI at $500M (Series A). Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied; while the sector median P/S across public semiconductors stands at 20.3x, Porotech's revenue is not disclosed, precluding reliable extrapolation.

The triangulated range spans $34M (floor) to $1.1B (ceiling), with a $520M median across both applicable methods. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around Porotech's technology maturity, addressable market capture potential, and nearness to revenue inflection relative to its peer cohort. The stage-progression floor is substantially conservative; upside toward peer medians depends on demonstrable technical differentiation and path-to-margin visibility.
